content.text:
{"tests":[{"filename":"001_interactive_n_declines_overwrite.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
set +e
printf 'n\n' | "$UTIL" -i "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "mv unexpectedly failed" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ "$(cat "$tmpdir/dst")" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "destination was overwritten despite negative interactive response" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"-i prompts before overwrite and a negative response must prevent the overwrite, even when stdin is a pipe; impl auto-confirms when stdin is not a tty","expected":"destination remains with its original contents after answering n to the interactive overwrite prompt","expected_to_fail":false,"manpage_quote":"-i, --interactive
              prompt before overwrite"},{"filename":"002_interactive_eof_declines_overwrite.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" -i "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" </dev/null >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "mv unexpectedly failed" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ "$(cat "$tmpdir/dst")" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "destination was overwritten despite EOF at interactive prompt" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"-i prompts before overwrite; EOF provides no affirmative response, but impl treats non-tty stdin as yes","expected":"destination remains unchanged when interactive overwrite receives EOF","expected_to_fail":false,"manpage_quote":"-i, --interactive
              prompt before overwrite"},{"filename":"003_final_i_after_f_controls_prompt.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
set +e
printf 'n\n' | "$UTIL" -f -i "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "mv unexpectedly failed" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ "$(cat "$tmpdir/dst")" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "final -i did not prevent overwrite after negative response" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"when -f and -i are both specified, final -i takes effect and prompts before overwrite; impl still auto-confirms on non-tty stdin","expected":"destination remains unchanged after -f -i and an n response","expected_to_fail":false,"manpage_quote":"-i, --interactive
              prompt before overwrite

       -n, --no-clobber
              do not overwrite an existing file

       If you specify more than one of -i, -f, -n, only the final one takes
       effect."},{"filename":"004_strip_trailing_slashes_moves_symlink_itself.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir "$tmpdir/real"
printf 'payload\n' > "$tmpdir/real/file"
ln -s "$tmpdir/real" "$tmpdir/link"
set +e
"$UTIL" --strip-trailing-slashes "$tmpdir/link/" "$tmpdir/moved" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "mv unexpectedly failed" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ ! -L "$tmpdir/moved" ]]; then
  echo "stripped symlink source was not moved as a symlink" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"--strip-trailing-slashes removes trailing slashes from SOURCE before moving, including a symlink source; impl deliberately keeps slash on symlink operands","expected":"destination is a symbolic link moved from the slash-stripped source operand","expected_to_fail":false,"manpage_quote":"--strip-trailing-slashes
              remove any trailing slashes from each SOURCE argument"},{"filename":"005_strip_trailing_slashes_does_not_apply_to_dest.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'payload\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
set +e
"$UTIL" --strip-trailing-slashes "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/newname/" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "destination trailing slash was stripped even though only SOURCE slashes are documented" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"--strip-trailing-slashes applies to SOURCE arguments only; impl maps the stripping function over all operands including DEST","expected":"mv fails rather than silently creating the destination with its trailing slash removed","expected_to_fail":true,"manpage_quote":"--strip-trailing-slashes
              remove any trailing slashes from each SOURCE argument"},{"filename":"006_backup_existing_detects_high_numbered_backup.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'prior\n' > "$tmpdir/dst.~5~"
set +e
"$UTIL" --backup=existing "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "mv unexpectedly failed" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ ! -e "$tmpdir/dst.~6~" ]]; then
  echo "existing numbered backup did not select next numbered backup" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"--backup=existing uses numbered backups if numbered backups already exist; impl only detects a preexisting .~1~ backup","expected":"old destination is backed up as the next numbered backup, dst.~6~","expected_to_fail":false,"manpage_quote":"existing, nil
              numbered if numbered backups exist, simple otherwise"},{"filename":"007_backup_suffix_override_simple_backup.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" --backup=simple --suffix=.bak "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "mv unexpectedly failed" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ ! -e "$tmpdir/dst.bak" ]]; then
  echo "custom backup suffix was not used" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"--suffix overrides the usual backup suffix for simple backups","expected":"existing destination is backed up using the .bak suffix","expected_to_fail":false,"manpage_quote":"The backup suffix is '~', unless set with --suffix or
       SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX."},{"filename":"008_backup_numbered_creates_next_number.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'prior\n' > "$tmpdir/dst.~1~"
set +e
"$UTIL" --backup=numbered "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "mv unexpectedly failed" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ ! -e "$tmpdir/dst.~2~" ]]; then
  echo "numbered backup did not create next numbered name" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"--backup=numbered makes numbered backups","expected":"existing destination is backed up as dst.~2~ when dst.~1~ already exists","expected_to_fail":false,"manpage_quote":"numbered, t
              make numbered backups"},{"filename":"009_update_older_skips_newer_destination.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
touch -t 202001010000 "$tmpdir/src"
touch -t 202201010000 "$tmpdir/dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" --update "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "mv unexpectedly failed" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ "$(cat "$tmpdir/dst")" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "newer destination was replaced under --update=older" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"--update with no argument defaults to older and replaces only when destination is older than source","expected":"newer destination remains unchanged under --update","expected_to_fail":false,"manpage_quote":"'older' is the default operation when --update is specified, and
       results in files being replaced if they're older than the corresponding
       source file."},{"filename":"010_update_all_default_replaces_even_newer_destination.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
touch -t 202001010000 "$tmpdir/src"
touch -t 202201010000 "$tmpdir/dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "mv unexpectedly failed" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ "$(cat "$tmpdir/dst")" != "new" ]]; then
  echo "default update=all did not replace existing destination" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"without --update, update mode all replaces existing destination files regardless of timestamps","expected":"destination is replaced by the source contents","expected_to_fail":false,"manpage_quote":"'all' is the default operation when an --update option is not
       specified, and results in all existing files in the destination being
       replaced."},{"filename":"011_update_none_fail_existing_destination_errors.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" --update=none-fail "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "--update=none-fail skipped replacement without failing" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"--update=none-fail diagnoses skipped existing destinations and induces failure","expected":"mv exits nonzero when an existing destination is skipped under --update=none-fail","expected_to_fail":true,"manpage_quote":"'none-fail' also ensures no files are replaced in the
       destination, but any skipped files are diagnosed and induce a failure."},{"filename":"012_invalid_update_value_errors.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
set +e
"$UTIL" --update=bogus "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "invalid --update value was accepted" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"--update accepts only the documented UPDATE values","expected":"mv exits nonzero for an invalid --update value","expected_to_fail":true,"manpage_quote":"UPDATE={all,none,none-fail,older(default)}"},{"filename":"013_target_directory_must_be_directory.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'payload\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'not a directory\n' > "$tmpdir/notdir"
set +e
"$UTIL" -t "$tmpdir/notdir" "$tmpdir/src" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "-t accepted a non-directory target" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"-t/--target-directory requires a DIRECTORY target","expected":"mv exits nonzero when -t names a regular file instead of a directory","expected_to_fail":true,"manpage_quote":"-t, --target-directory=DIRECTORY
              move all SOURCE arguments into DIRECTORY"},{"filename":"014_no_target_directory_treats_dest_as_file.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'payload\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
mkdir "$tmpdir/destdir"
set +e
"$UTIL" -T "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/destdir" >/dev/null 2>"$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "-T moved source into directory instead of treating DEST as a normal file" >&2
  exit 1
fi
","exercises":"-T treats DEST as a normal file rather than as a target directory","expected":"mv exits nonzero when asked with -T to replace an existing directory with a regular file","expected_to_fail":true,"manpage_quote":"-T, --no-target-directory
              treat DEST as a normal file"}]}
